learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the key concepts of the five subjects (Dance, Drama, Music, Media Arts, and Visual Arts) of the Arts as a key Learning Area in primary schools. (APST: 2.1)

Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the creative Arts learning and teaching processes that respond to children as confident and creative cultural citizens and artists from diverse backgrounds, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander, linguistic, cultural, religious and socioeconomic. (APST: 1.3, 1.4, 2.1)
Demonstrate knowledge of appropriate and effective literacy and numeracy teaching strategies in Arts-based pedagogies. (APST: 2.5)

Critically analyse the significance of the Arts as creative, embodied and aesthetic practice in primary school education, and how Arts education can be used to develop and enhance understandings of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander histories, cultures and languages. (APST: 2.1, 2.4)

Demonstrate knowledge, understanding and skills for designing, implementing, resourcing and evaluating authentic Arts education programs for the primary curriculum, with a focus on the implementation of appropriate teaching strategies that support inclusive student participation in learning. (APST: 2.2, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 4.1)

Demonstrate an understanding of and ability to use curriculum content knowledge, and assessment and reporting knowledge and strategies to effectively plan and assess Arts learning sequences appropriate for current primary curriculum and assessment policies. (APST: 2.3, 5.1, 5.2)
Demonstrate clear, fluent and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.

assessments.mode: Activity/ Performance, Product/ Artefact/ Multimedia, Written
assessments.task_description:
Across the semester in weeks you will be given an unseen task to complete in your tutorial. The in-class tutorial activities relate to the commensurate topic for that week. All tasks will be handwritten and submitted to your tutor before the end of the tutorial. The tasks will be either knowledge-based, reflective or skills-based. They are pass/fail. Students must successfully complete 3 out of the 5 in-class tutorial tasks in order to pass the course overall. 

The university is transitioning all courses to have a “secure” item of assessment. This task conforms to this requirement.  

You MUST successfully complete and submit 3 out of the possible 5 in-class tutorial activities in order to pass this course overall. 

The tutorial tasks will require you to: 

  • Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the interrelated concepts of the five subjects of the Arts: Dance, Drama, Music, Media Arts, and Visual Arts, as a key Learning Area in Primary education (APST 2.1); and 
  • Critically analyse the significance of the Arts as creative, embodied and aesthetic practice in primary school education, and how Arts education can be used to develop and enhance understandings of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander histories, cultures and languages (APST 2.4).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: These tasks need to be completed within your tutorial for secure assessment purposes. As such, there is no late submission avenue available.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Plan and critically reflect on a curriculum project that embeds Learning Objectives 1 – 7. 

Part A: Unit Plan 40% 

You are a primary school teacher who is planning a unit of work that will focus on an Arts area to provide arts-rich learning opportunities for your students. Choose a year level and an Arts area, and complete the unit of work using the provided template. Your unit plan should include authentic Arts learning experiences appropriate for your chosen year level and draw on theoretically informed pedagogical approaches to Arts education. Connecting clearly to at least one of the General Capabilities and one Cross-curriculum priority, your unit should offer a range of Arts learning experiences and assessment tasks that are tailored to meet the diverse needs of students in your class.  

The unit plan will include: 

  • a meaningful title that reflects the focus or theme of the unit The Arts area your unit of work addresses 
  • the year level and class and one or two sentences describing diverse needs of students.  
  • one or two sentences outlining students' prior knowledge/engagement with the unit focus or theme, approximate timeframe and number of lessons) and the integrated learning area.  
  • one or two sentences providing an overview of assessment (including summative task), content description codes for the Arts area and the Arts strands addressed in the unit.the aims and  
  • one or two sentences that provide a snapshot overview of the unit. 

In your plan, you should also include: 

  • brief dot point descriptions of the General Capabilities and Cross Curriculum Priorities that have been embedded in the learning experiences  
  • dot point outlines of the teaching and learning sequence, including the teaching context, activities (including differentiation), knowledge and skills (e.g., Arts elements, principles, conventions), assessment tasks. A 
  • a range of resources, including ICTs and at least one item held in the UQ Anthropology Museum Collection, UQ Art Museum Collection, and/or Fryer Library. 
  • two formative assessment tasks and one summative assessment task to show how the Arts subject achievement standard, will be assessed and reported to inform creative processes. 

Part B Critical Reflection 20% 

Drawing on examples in your unit plan, write a critical analysis of, and rationale for, the choices you made in planning your unit. You should include the following areas:  

1) Arts education research; 

2) Key Arts educational theories/philosophies; 

3) Arts curriculum content 

Your reflection should also include, where relevant, how you have considered the following in developing your unit plan: 

  • creative processes;  
  • teaching contexts;  
  • protocols for First Nations intellectual and cultural property (ICIP);  
  • learner diversity; materials, technologies, and forms;  
  • immersive technologies; 
  • viewpoints 

In your critical reflection, you should draw on research literature, course readings and any relevant curriculum documents, education policy, declaration/s, and Indigenous Cultural and Intellectual Property (ICIP). 

(APST 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 4.1, 5.1)
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment task should be submitted electronically via the link in the Assessment folder on blackboard.

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Task 3 is a written assignment which critically explores the creative, embodied and/or aesthetic dimensions to Arts education from a philosophical viewpoint. 

As a becoming Arts educator, it is important to understand and consider what, how and why your theoretical understandings of Arts education are connected to your Arts teaching practices.  

Drawing on the readings and ideas you have been introduced to in this course and beyond, choose one or a group of Arts education philosophers and write an essay describing:  

  1. How Arts education philosophy contributes to awareness of self as a creative and cultural citizen; 
  2. How Arts education philosophy contributes to the development of learning and teaching strategies that are responsive to students from a broad range of backgrounds and enhance inclusive participation in Arts learning;  
  3. How Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander ontologies develop and enhance understandings of and respect for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander histories, cultures and languages through the Arts, and how these can impact on the education of students.  

FAQs regarding Assessment 2: 

1) you decide the format 

2) you may use subheadings 

3) you may use first person 

4) your essay should draw on course readings as well as other peer-reviewed publications 

5) quotes contribute to the word count so keep them to a minimum 

6) 10% +/- wiggle room on word count 

7) no screenshots or dot points, just academic prose 

8) all citations must be from peer reviewed publications  

9) APA 7th for referencing.   

Referencing:   

APA - https://guides.library.uq.edu.au/referencing/apa7 

(APST 1.3, 1.4, 2.1, 2.4, 4.1)
